status: RE-RUN 2026-07-09. BSBM 200M (pc=570000) throughput matrix on the m7a.4xlarge box, Fluree v4.1.2 (13a78d2a). Real BSBM: bsbmtools-0.2 test driver (randomized query mix -> QMpH). Explore + BusinessIntelligence (update omitted at scale) x {1,4,8,16,32} clients, 0 timeouts. vs the 25d8c28f baseline: Explore +11-31%, BI flat-to-better (+1-6%). BI ran all 5 client counts this time (server restarted before each BI cell for cache headroom); 0 timeouts vs the baseline's 1.

dataset.name: BSBM 200M (pc=570000, forward-chained)
dataset.description: Berlin SPARQL Benchmark e-commerce dataset, 200M stress scale point. Deterministic generator (no seed); pin = toolkit SHA + -pc + flags.

methodology.notes: BI ran all 5 client counts. To avoid the OOM the earlier baseline hit (heavy -fc root-type ProductType1 analytic query working set on top of a filled ~15 GiB LeafletCache exceeding the 64 GB box), the server is RESTARTED before each BI cell so it starts with full cache headroom. All 10 cells (explore + bi) completed with 0 timeouts. BI throughput is dominated by the -fc root-type draw (full-scan analytic queries), a known generator/methodology artifact, not an engine ceiling.

fluree.label: Fluree
fluree.install: Built from source (cargo build --release -p fluree-db-cli -p fluree-db-server). Binary reports 'fluree 4.1.1' (pre-release build of v4.1.2).
fluree.config: default server config; LeafletCache budget ~21.5 GiB

caveats: Real BSBM via bsbmtools-0.2 (randomized query mix, QMpH).

BI@200M can exceed 64 GB RAM when a root-type (ProductType1) analytic query lands on top of a filled leaflet cache -> server OOM. The 2026-07-09 run restarts the server before each BI cell (full cache headroom) and completes all 5 client counts with 0 timeouts. Other workarounds: more RAM, smaller cache budget, or exclude the root type from the param pool / regenerate without -fc.
Absolute QMpH is comparable engine-vs-engine on this box, not across toolkit versions/scales.

200M explore measured on the warmed ledger (warm-up pass then w3 r30); run-to-run variance on the 49GB ledger is ~7%, so small explore deltas at 200M are within noise (the +11-31% vs baseline is well outside it).
